one_time_description: Equity in gain from government business enterprises and partnerships, primarily driven by forgiveness of intercompany loans within GBEs (Acho Camps & Catering and Acho Real Estate limited partnerships)
economic_notes: First Nation has investments in multiple government business enterprises and partnerships including fuel, construction, transportation, real estate, petroleum, camps/catering, and air operations. ADK Petroleum LP active in oil and gas exploration on traditional lands in NT, BC and Yukon. Beaver Enterprises LP active in construction, transportation and repairs with $5.4M revenue. Liard Fuel Centre LP operates gas station and fuel sales with $2.1M revenue.
notes: Audit is qualified due to GBE/GBP year ends (May 31) not coinciding with First Nation year end (March 31); GBEs reported using modified equity method at May 31, 2022 figures. GBEs use ASPE instead of IFRS as required by PSAS. Large equity gain of $3,596,693 from GBEs primarily driven by forgiveness of intercompany loans. First Nation is economically dependent on Government of Canada and GNWT. No long-term debt on First Nation balance sheet. Contributions receivable of $1,939,428 is a significant asset. Ongoing litigation including Federal Court Judicial Reviews. ISC funding estimated from contributions receivable detail; total government transfers calculated from identified government revenue lines (Canada $1,817,155 + GNWT $503,267 + Yukon $30,006 + BC Treaty Commission $150,000 + Dehcho First Nation $40,000 = $2,540,428). No schedule of remuneration disclosed in provided text.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
